topic: Too much self-reflection is linked to anxiety and depression.

framework.structure: ['The reason you'] + [Universal Relatable Behaviour] + ['is not'] + [Assumed Reason] + ['it is'] + [Dark Psychological Mechanism]
framework.trigger: Self-revelation — reframes a familiar experience with a disturbing explanation the viewer cannot unsee
framework.example: The reason you can't stop scrolling is not boredom. It is a dopamine loop engineered by behavioural scientists.

script.hook: The reason you can't stop analyzing yourself is not self-improvement. It is your brain weaponizing introspection against you.
script.body: Every thought you examine creates neural pathways that loop back on themselves. You're not gaining clarity—you're building anxiety spirals. The more you dissect, the more broken you feel.
script.cta: Follow before you realize how deep this goes.

full_script_text: The reason you can't stop analyzing yourself is not self-improvement. It is your brain weaponizing introspection against you. Every thought you examine creates neural pathways that loop back on themselves. You're not gaining clarity—you're building anxiety spirals. The more you dissect, the more broken you feel. Follow before you realize how deep this goes.

visual_direction.kling_prompt: extreme close-up eyes in deep shadow, single side light source, no full faces, 9:16 vertical cinematic, psychological thriller, deep shadows
